reviews.review_msg: Nice guy, easy course. Would definitely take another course with him.

Much of your success on the midterms and in essays, as with most philosophy classes, predicates on whether you're able to engage HIS lines of reasoning in the written questions. This kind of philosophy relies especially on whether you can defeat an argument on its own terms. Read the book to understand, go to lecture to strategize for exams.

He's very nice. Midterm1 and midterm2 are super easy. If you go to class, you can get at least A- on the midterms. MC on the final are hard and very confused. If you put effort into this class, you can get at least B for the overall grade

Well-structured and organized course with fair exams. Genuinely nice guy who is happy to help out if you have questions. Not condescending either, which is rare in this department :) Make sure to stay on top of the course material and attend lectures, as lecture outlines aren't always provided.  Definitely recommend this course.

Nice guy, decent class. Found his lectures almost useless, as well as the textbooks. The examples he gives are good, but I found he didn't give a clear idea of the arguments and the lack of lecture material didn't help. Hard to follow scribbles on the board with his oral lecture. Test were easy, but the multiple choice was stupid.
He's a nice guy. He is helpful, and replies emails in a short time and answers questions clearly. This course is definitely the easiest course i've ever taken!
He's a really nice prof. He's really helpful. He replied emails in a short time, and answers questions clearly. Definitely the easiest course I've ever taken at SFU.

Super easy course!Textbook is not so expensive, useful but not really required after midterm.One of his TA is harder maker.But all the things all good,Midterms are easy, of MC in final little bit hard.  easy A!
A nice guy. The content itself is pretty easy but the class is boring. You don't have to attend the lectures if you read the textbook yourself.
